Australia's Sigma Healthcare has dropped its pursuit of UK pharmacy chain Boots, saying a deal for the 1,800-store business - estimated to be worth $10bn - would not meet its strategic and capital investment objectives; Sigma shares jumped 6% on the news.

Anthropic's suspension of its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models for all foreign nationals has reignited debate in India over sovereign AI, with investor Mohandas Pai calling for a 500 billion rupee annual fund for AI and deep tech alongside a 2 trillion rupee credit guarantee programme.

Starmer pledged £210m for Ukraine's nuclear plants for the next two years at the G7, saying the investment through UK Export Finance in uranium enrichment company Urenco would supply enriched uranium to Ukraine's nuclear power producer Energoatom.

SpaceX stock climbed 20% on Monday, its first full day of full trading, with roughly 244 million shares changing hands; CEO Elon Musk posted on X that the company "might be able to reach approximately" $1 trillion revenue in 2030, up from $18.7 billion last year.

The US says Iran nuclear talks will begin after the formal signing of the framework agreement on June 19, with the White House emphasising the memorandum of understanding is only a framework, not a final peace deal, and that sanctions relief will be tied to inspections.

UK unemployment fell to 4.9% in the three months to April while wage growth including bonuses climbed to 4.4%, with vacancies slumping to their lowest level since the three months to April 2021 at 707,000, according to ONS figures.

Amazon founder Jeff Bezos told VivaTech Paris that AI will create "a labour shortage" rather than mass redundancies, in direct contradiction of figures like former UK prime minister Rishi Sunak who has said AI is already affecting young people's job prospects.
